cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why small pieces of space junk can threaten satellites. The options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to look back at the text to find the relevant information. The text mentions that even bits of space junk as small as dots of paint pose a threat. Then it says that the debris orbits at speeds up to 28,200 km per hour. The high speed is explained as the reason why tiny paint dots have damaged space shuttles' windshields so badly they had to be replaced. So the key point here is the speed. The high speed makes even small pieces dangerous because when they hit something, the impact is greater. So the answer is probably A. Let me check the other options. Option B says they're hard to track, but the text doesn't mention tracking being the problem. It's more about the speed. Option C says they can blow up into millions of pieces, but the text doesn't say that the small pieces themselves explode. The problem is from their speed, not their multiplication.
